title: Hieracium ucenicum
taxonomy:
I can find some information regarding Hieracium ucenicum classification. 
Hieracium ucenicum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the genus Hieracium, commonly known as hawkweeds. More specifically, within the family Asteraceae, it belongs to the subfamily Cichorioideae and tribe Cichorieae.

intro: Hieracium ucenicum, also known as the Ucenic hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion-like group of plants in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce pain, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium ucenicum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as in the form of dried leaves and flowers for medicinal purposes.
